stories.title: Iraqi officials, including lawmakers, arrested on corruption charges in overnight raid
stories.summary:
Iraqi officials, including lawmakers, arrested on corruption charges in overnight raid. Dozens of Iraqi political officials have been arrested on corruption charges, Iraq's state-run Iraqi News Agency reported Sunday.

Al Jazeera: Iraq arrests senior officials in anti-corruption raids.

Al Jazeera reports that iraqi security forces arrested several politicians, lawmakers and senior officials in dawn raids across Baghdad.

stories.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
stories.summary:
Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border. A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

Al Jazeera: Three firefighters killed in US wildfires along Colorado–Utah border.

Al Jazeera reports that the US has seen a harsh wildfire season this year, with prolonged droughts and dry conditions fuelling blazes.

stories.title: In Lebanon, framework agreement signed with Israel spurs protest, criticism
stories.summary:
In Lebanon, framework agreement signed with Israel spurs protest, criticism. Anger over deal that does not force Israeli troops to withdraw from occupied land after months of deadly attacks.

BBC: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal.

BBC reports that strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.summary:
Bill Maher Is Not Down With The ‘Jew Hating’ Socialists: ‘My Vote Is In Play’. Comedian and HBO host Bill Maher admitted on Friday that his vote would be “in play” in coming elections if the Democratic Party continued its headlong gallop toward socialism.

stories.title: American Tributes – Ted Budd: 'The Founding Fathers Rejected Overreach and Overregulation'
stories.summary: American Tributes – Ted Budd: 'The Founding Fathers Rejected Overreach and Overregulation'. In a video for Breitbart News's American Tributes project, Sen. Ted Budd (R-NC) spoke about how the United States "honors tradition and embraces innovation." Budd explained how the same spirit which led the Founding Fathers to reject "overreach and over regulation" is still driving the nation today, and pointed to how the state of North Carolina was thriving and was a "national leader in innovation." stories.title: How tourists and plastic transformed Bali into an 'Island of Trash'
stories.summary: How tourists and plastic transformed Bali into an 'Island of Trash'. Known to many as the Island of the Gods, residents fear Bali may soon become the "Island of Trash" due to a deluge of tourists, a lack of rubbish education and the partial closure of a much-needed landfill facility.

stories.title: Lost your crypto access code? Be wary, there‘s a scam for that too
stories.summary:
Lost your crypto access code? Be wary, there‘s a scam for that too.

A niche type of fraud is lucrative enough for criminals to set up fake websites with dodgy software to harvest your data After holding them for a few years, you have decided it is time to cash in your cryptocurrency holdings.

The problem is, it is so long since you set up the digital wallet which manages them on your laptop, you have forgotten the lengthy access code. Stressed at the thought of losing thousands of pounds, you search and download a program which promises to recover.

stories.title: ‘You can’t make billions without hurting people’: Cory Doctorow on Elon Musk, the AI bubble and bosses’ cruel fantasies
stories.summary: ‘You can’t make billions without hurting people’: Cory Doctorow on Elon Musk, the AI bubble and bosses’ cruel fantasies. The writer who coined the word ‘enshittification’ tells us why AI will never deliver what it promises – and why it still appeals so much to those in power A “centaur”, in automation theory, is a person assisted by a machine, and a “reverse centaur”, hero of Cory Doctorow’s new book, The Reverse Centaur’s Guide to Life After.

stories.title: 3 nabbed for illegal mining, P5-M equipment seized in Surigao del Sur
stories.summary:
3 nabbed for illegal mining, P5-M equipment seized in Surigao del Sur. MANILA, Philippines — Three individuals were arrested in Barobo, Surigao del Sur for failing to present proper documentation for mining activities, the Philippine National Police said on Sunday.

Police made the arrests and confiscated equipment worth P5,092,000, on Saturday.

stories.title: Negros Occidental LGUs intensify biosecurity measures to ward off ASF
stories.summary:
Negros Occidental LGUs intensify biosecurity measures to ward off ASF.

BACOLOD CITY, NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, Philippines — To protect the multi-million peso local livestock industry and secure the livelihoods of small-scale and commercial hog raisers, local government units (LGUs) in Negros Occidental have scaled up their biosecurity defenses against African Swine Fever (ASF).

Through a mix of strict border inspections, temporary bans, and localized task forces,.

stories.summary:

stories.title: Soldier nabbed for firing rifle outside brother-in-law’s house in Cagayan
stories.summary:
Soldier nabbed for firing rifle outside brother-in-law’s house in Cagayan. TUGUEGARAO CITY, Philippines— A 28-year-old Army corporal was arrested for allegedly firing his high-powered firearm outside his brother-in-law’s house in Barangay Luzon, Claveria, Cagayan, in the early hours of Sunday, June 28, police said.

Col. Mardito Angoluan, director of the Cagayan Police Provincial Office, identified the suspect only by his alias, Bugoy, from Calanasan, Apayao.
